findings.title: Cookie missing HttpOnly flags
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Cookie is missing security flags that protect against common attacks.

**Analysis:**
🔐 remember-me/login cookie - high value target
❌ Missing: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite
findings.why_it_matters:
• **HttpOnly** prevents XSS attacks from stealing cookies via JavaScript
• **Secure** ensures cookies are only sent over HTTPS
• **SameSite** prevents CSRF attacks by controlling cross-site requests

findings.title: Express app missing helmet
findings.description:
Helmet sets important security headers to protect against common attacks.

**App Analysis:**
📊 Security Score: 20%
🛣️ Routes: 9
❌ Missing: helmet, rate-limit, body-parser-limit
findings.why_it_matters:
Without helmet, your app is missing:
• X-Content-Type-Options (prevents MIME sniffing)
• X-Frame-Options (prevents clickjacking)
• X-XSS-Protection (legacy XSS filter)
• Strict-Transport-Security (enforces HTTPS)
• Content-Security-Policy (prevents XSS)

findings.title: Callback hell (6 levels deep)
findings.description:
Deeply nested callbacks (6 levels) make code hard to follow.

**Analysis:**
⚠️ 58 anonymous functions - consider naming them
findings.suggested_fix:
Refactor options:
1. Convert to async/await (recommended)
2. Extract nested callbacks into named functions
3. Use Promise.all() for parallel operations
findings.why_it_matters: The 'pyramid of doom' hurts readability and makes error handling difficult. Each nesting level increases cognitive load.

findings.title: XSS: innerHTML assignment
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] **Direct innerHTML assignment can lead to XSS vulnerabilities** **Location**: test/res.redirect.js:115 **Code snippet**: ``` var xss = 'javascript:eval(document.body.innerHTML=`

XSS

`);'; ``` Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities occur when untrusted data is included in web pages without proper validation or escaping. Attackers can inject malicious scripts that: - Steal user session cookies - Capture keystrokes and credentials - Redirect users to malicious sites - Deface the application This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

findings.title: XSS: innerHTML assignment
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] **Direct innerHTML assignment can lead to XSS vulnerabilities**

**Location**: test/res.redirect.js:116

**Code snippet**:
```
var encodedXss = 'javascript:eval(document.body.innerHTML=%60%3Cp%3EXSS%3C/p%3E%60);';
```

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities occur when untrusted data is included
in web pages without proper validation or escaping. Attackers can inject malicious
scripts that:
- Steal user session cookies
- Capture keystrokes and credentials
- Redirect users to malicious sites
- Deface the application

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of
Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

findings.title: Express app missing rate limiting
findings.description:
Rate limiting prevents brute force attacks and DoS.

**App Analysis:**
📊 Security Score: 20%
🛣️ Routes: 9
❌ Missing: helmet, rate-limit, body-parser-limit
findings.why_it_matters:
Without rate limiting, attackers can:
• Brute force passwords
• Scrape data at scale
• DoS your API
• Abuse expensive endpoints

findings.title: Path traversal in file download
findings.description: [Non-production path] [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] File download/send function with user-controlled path. Attackers could download arbitrary files from the server.
findings.suggested_fix: Use res.download() with { root: '/safe/base/dir' } option, or validate resolved path is within allowed directory.
findings.why_it_matters: Attackers could download sensitive configuration files, source code, or credentials from the server.

findings.title: Global mutable variable: users
findings.description:
Global mutable state 'users' makes code hard to reason about.

**Impact Analysis:**
📊 Used 17 times in this file
findings.suggested_fix:
Many usages - consider:
1. Encapsulate in a module with getter/setter
2. Convert to a class instance
3. Pass as parameter instead
findings.why_it_matters: Global state causes hidden dependencies between functions. Changes to globals can have unexpected effects throughout the codebase.

findings.title: Callback hell (5 levels deep)
findings.description:
Deeply nested callbacks (5 levels) make code hard to follow.

**Analysis:**
⚠️ 132 anonymous functions - consider naming them
findings.suggested_fix:
Refactor options:
1. Convert to async/await (recommended)
2. Extract nested callbacks into named functions
3. Use Promise.all() for parallel operations
findings.why_it_matters: The 'pyramid of doom' hurts readability and makes error handling difficult. Each nesting level increases cognitive load.

findings.title: String concatenation in loop
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Variable 'str' concatenated 2 times inside loop (started line 186).

**Performance:** O(n²) time complexity. Each concatenation creates a new string and copies all previous characters.

For 1000 iterations, this copies ~500,000 characters instead of 1000.
findings.why_it_matters: String concatenation in loops creates O(n²) time complexity due to immutable string copying.

Catching generic Exception hides bugs and makes debugging difficult.

**Analysis:**
📞 Try block calls: statSync
findings.suggested_fix: Catch specific exceptions instead of generic Exception.
findings.why_it_matters: Broad exception catches mask unexpected errors like TypeErrors or AttributeErrors that indicate bugs in your code.

findings.title: Callback hell (4 levels deep)
findings.description:
Deeply nested callbacks (4 levels) make code hard to follow.

**Analysis:**
⚠️ 52 anonymous functions - consider naming them
findings.suggested_fix:
Refactor options:
1. Convert to async/await (recommended)
2. Extract nested callbacks into named functions
3. Use Promise.all() for parallel operations
findings.why_it_matters: The 'pyramid of doom' hurts readability and makes error handling difficult. Each nesting level increases cognitive load.

findings.title: Broad exception catch
findings.description:
Catching generic Exception hides bugs and makes debugging difficult.

**Analysis:**
📞 Try block calls: SyntaxError, parse
findings.suggested_fix:
Based on the operations in your try block, consider catching:
  • SyntaxError (for JSON.parse)

Example:
```python
except (SyntaxError (for JSON.parse)) as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Broad exception catches mask unexpected errors like TypeErrors or AttributeErrors that indicate bugs in your code.
